user_story: As an analyst I undo a bad import

errors: DEFECT (not fixed, moderate scope): the 20 per-format routes (/import-thor, /import-wazuh, etc.) never populate import-meta or push an undo checkpoint — only the generic /import and /import-file handlers do. Reproduced on all 20. Not user-facing (the dashboard only ever calls the unified /import), but a real gap for any API/extension/script caller of a per-format endpoint expecting undo/import-meta parity. Root cause: src/routes/import.ts — only the generic and /import-file handlers contain the stateBefore->diff->importMetaStore.record->pushImportCheckpoint chain; each per-format handler ends with a bare pipeline call + resynthesize, no diff/checkpoint code.
fix: NOT auto-fixed — 20 call sites, needs a deliberate refactor (extract the diff/checkpoint chain from the generic /import handler into a shared helper, call it from all 20) rather than a one-line patch. Recommend as a follow-up task.

user_story: As an analyst I view the causal attack graph
expected_behaviour: process trees + lateral movement + file lineage + network flows (derived)
test_result: LIVE DEFECT: GET /evidence-graph 200 (no crash, concurrency fix holds) but ZERO lateral_move edges despite an unambiguous textbook 3-host PsExec lateral-movement chain in the demo seed data (events e012/e017/e018).
errors: Root cause (src/analysis/evidenceGraph.ts:132-181): the hash-based lateral-move rule requires a shared sha256/md5 across hosts, but the demo's PsExec events carry no hash field. The account-based rule requires a strict DOMAIN\user or UPN regex match (assetGraph.ts extractAccounts), but the events describe accounts in prose ("using domain admin credentials", "account: jsmith") with no DOMAIN\ prefix, so it never extracts a token either. The panel's entire stated value proposition (lateral movement via shared hashes/accounts) silently omits the case's most obvious lateral-movement evidence with no error or indication anything is missing.
fix: NOT auto-fixed — this is a detection-heuristic/algorithm change (blast radius: affects every case's Evidence Chain panel), not a mechanical bug fix. Needs a product decision: either importers should populate structured account fields on lateral-movement events, or the heuristic should also key off the network_flow srcIp->dstIp:445 cross-host pairs it already computes (visible in the same response) as a lower-confidence lateral-movement signal.

Now 422 when nothing enabled (regression test added), matching the already-correct sibling bulk-enrich route.
errors: Route built its 202 response from the full server-configured provider list, not the per-case enabled subset; enrichInBackground checked enabledProvidersFor(caseId) and silently returned when empty — analyst got a false "will enrich N IOCs with M providers" response and nothing happened.
fix: src/routes/threatIntel.ts POST /cases/:id/enrich now calls ctx.enabledProvidersFor(caseId) before responding: 422 "no enrichment providers enabled for this case" when empty, real effective provider list in the 202 body otherwise. Regression test added in tests/server.test.ts.

user_story: As an analyst I push the report to Notion
expected_behaviour: POST /cases/:id/push/notion; /notion/status
test_result: LIVE DEFECT FOUND + FIXED: POST /push/notion with {} body returned 502 "Notion rejected the request: Invalid request URL." because the env-configured database ID (a full Notion URL) was never parsed to a bare id.
errors: src/server.ts notionPushOptions() set databaseId: process.env.DFIR_NOTION_DATABASE_ID with no parsing, while the request-body page/parent/database flow (routes/caseLifecycle.ts) already runs the same kind of value through parseNotionPageId() to extract the UUID from arbitrary Notion URL formats. The env-derived default bypassed that normalization.
fix: notionPushOptions() now applies parseNotionPageId() to DFIR_NOTION_DATABASE_ID / DFIR_NOTION_PARENT_PAGE_ID, matching the body-input code path. Regression test added in tests/server/notionPushOptions.test.ts.

user_story: As an analyst I manually fix the asset graph
expected_behaviour: GET /cases/:id/asset-overrides; add/delete assets, links, restore
test_result: LIVE DEFECT FOUND + FIXED: renaming a manually-added asset persisted in the overrides store but was silently dropped from the asset-graph projection. Regression test added.
errors: src/analysis/assetOverrides.ts applyAssetOverrides() step 1 (auto-derived assets) applies overrides.renames[a.id], but step 2 (manually-added assets) used ma.name directly and never consulted overrides.renames[ma.id]. Secondary: deleting a manual asset left an orphaned rename key in storage indefinitely.
fix: Step 2 now uses overrides.renames[ma.id] ?? ma.name, mirroring step 1. removeAsset() now also prunes the rename entry when deleting a manual asset (an auto-derived asset's rename is kept, since its id can reappear on a later synthesis). Regression test added in tests/analysis/assetOverrides.test.ts.

errors: Medium: route skipped isValidCaseId, unlike sibling password routes -> crafted caseId reached store.getCaseMeta before existence check (path traversal, narrow exploitability)

user_story: As an analyst I unlock a password-protected case to view it
expected_behaviour: POST /cases/:id/unlock verifies password (scrypt + timingSafeEqual) against stored salted hash; 400 invalid caseId; 401 wrong password; issues signed HttpOnly SameSite=strict cookie (1yr TTL if remember:true, else 12h session); no-op 200 if case has no password
test_result: LIVE re-verified: 401 wrong password, 200 correct + Set-Cookie HttpOnly;SameSite=Strict, 200 no-op no-password case, 400 invalid caseId — traversal fix confirmed live
errors: Medium (fixed): same isValidCaseId gap as lock-status. High/Medium open (deployment-dependent, NOT auto-fixed): no rate limiting or brute-force lockout on /unlock -- unthrottled guessing possible if server is ever bound beyond 127.0.0.1 (DFIR_HOST=0.0.0.0)
fix: path-traversal: isValidCaseId guard added. Rate limiting: NOT applied -- needs a policy decision (lockout threshold/duration, per-IP vs per-case) before implementing; recommend express-rate-limit or a per-case attempt counter on /unlock

errors: DEFECT (fixed): an empty {} JSON body was accepted as a valid "siem" event (202) instead of the documented 400 "empty push payload" — JSON.stringify({}) produces the non-empty string "{}", which slipped past the empty-payload guard and produced a junk timeline row.
